journal_entry.reason: Security deposit received in cash creates a refundable liability until lease termination or application to damages

output.reasoning: Transaction states rent is "exclusive of sales tax" but does not state a rate or amount. Rent is a taxable supply in Ontario (13% HST), but the text does not provide the tax rate or amount, only that it is excluded from the $10,000 figure.
output.tax_context: Rent is exclusive of sales tax. Ontario HST (13%) applies to the rental income. The security deposit is not subject to tax (it is a refundable deposit liability, not consideration for a supply).

asset_increase.reason: Cash received for security deposit from tenant

liability_increase.reason: Security deposit received from tenant to be held until lease termination

ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: Security deposits are recognized as a financial liability under IFRS 9 since they represent an obligation to return cash

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Security deposits are not subject to sales tax as they are refundable and not consideration for a supply
This transaction is only the receipt of the security deposit upon signing — no rent revenue is recognized yet as no rental period has occurred
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 16 (lessor accounting): rental income recognized on a straight-line basis over the lease term — no revenue at signing, only when rental periods occur
